mn152:1.2: At one time the Bhagavā was dwelling at Gajaṅgalā, in the Sweet Bamboo Grove.
mn152:10.1: And how, Ānanda, is a trainee one on the path?
mn152:10.10: Thus, Ānanda, a trainee is one on the path.
mn152:10.2: Here, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* sees a form with the eye, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:10.3: He is troubled, ashamed, and disgusted by that arisen liking, arisen disliking, and arisen both liking and disliking.
mn152:10.4: Having heard a sound with the ear …
mn152:10.5: having smelled an odor with the nose …
mn152:10.6: having tasted a flavor with the tongue …
mn152:10.7: having touched a tangible thing with the body …
mn152:10.8: when he cognizes a *dhamma* with the mind, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:10.9: He is troubled, ashamed, and disgusted by that arisen liking, arisen disliking, and arisen both liking and disliking.
mn152:11-15.1: And how, Ānanda, is a noble one one with developed *indriyas* [*bhāvitindriyo*]?
mn152:11-15.2: Here, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* sees a form with the eye, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:11-15.3: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the non-repulsive in the repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the non-repulsive.
mn152:11-15.4: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the repulsive in the non-repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the repulsive.
mn152:11-15.5: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the non-repulsive in both the repulsive and the non-repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the non-repulsive.
mn152:11-15.6: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the repulsive in both the non-repulsive and the repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the repulsive.
mn152:11-15.7: If he wishes, ‘Avoiding both the repulsive and the non-repulsive, may I dwell equanimous, with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*],’ he dwells there equanimous, with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*].
mn152:16.1: Again, Ānanda, having heard a sound with the ear …
mn152:16.10: If he wishes, ‘Avoiding both the repulsive and the non-repulsive, may I dwell equanimous, with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*],’ he dwells there equanimous, with *sati*, fully aware [*sampajāno*].
mn152:16.11: Thus, Ānanda, a noble one is one with developed *indriyas* [*bhāvitindriyo*].
mn152:16.2: having smelled an odor with the nose …
mn152:16.3: having tasted a flavor with the tongue …
mn152:16.4: having touched a tangible thing with the body …
mn152:16.5: when he cognizes a *dhamma* with the mind, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:16.6: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the non-repulsive in the repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the non-repulsive.
mn152:16.7: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the repulsive in the non-repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the repulsive.
mn152:16.8: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the non-repulsive in both the repulsive and the non-repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the non-repulsive.
mn152:16.9: If he wishes, ‘May I dwell perceiving the repulsive in both the non-repulsive and the repulsive,’ he dwells there perceiving the repulsive.
mn152:17.1: So, Ānanda, I have taught the unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya*; I have taught the trainee on the path; I have taught the noble one with developed *indriyas* [*bhāvitindriyo*].
mn152:18.1: What should be done by a teacher for disciples, seeking their welfare, compassionate, out of compassion, that has been done by me for you.
mn152:18.2: These are the roots of trees, Ānanda, these are empty huts. Meditate [*jhāyatha*], Ānanda, do not be negligent, do not later be remorseful. This is my instruction to you.”
mn152:18.3: This is what the Bhagavā said.
mn152:18.4: Venerable Ānanda was glad and approved what the Bhagavā said.
mn152:18.5: The Development of the *indriyas* [*Indriyabhāvanā*] is finished, the tenth.
mn152:18.6: The fifth chapter, on the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], is finished.
mn152:19.3: The Six Sets of Six, and on the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatanikaṁ*],
mn152:19.6: the chapter with exhortation as the fifth.
mn152:2.1: Then Uttara, the brahmin student of Pārāsivi, went to the Bhagavā. Having gone to the Bhagavā, he exchanged greetings with him.
mn152:2.10: For a blind person, Uttara, does not see a form with the eye, and a deaf person does not hear a sound with the ear.”
mn152:2.11: When this was said, Uttara, the brahmin student of Pārāsivi, sat silent, embarrassed, shoulders drooping, downcast, brooding, with nothing to say.
mn152:2.2: When the courteous and memorable talk was finished, he sat down to one side. Seated to one side, Uttara the brahmin student of Pārāsivi was addressed by the Bhagavā:
mn152:2.3: “Uttara, does the brahmin Pārāsivi teach his disciples development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*]?”
mn152:2.4: “He does, Master Gotama, the brahmin Pārāsivi teaches his disciples development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*].”
mn152:2.5: “But how, Uttara, does the brahmin Pārāsivi teach his disciples development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*]?”
mn152:2.6: “Here, Master Gotama, one does not see a form with the eye, one does not hear a sound with the ear—
mn152:2.7: that is how, Master Gotama, the brahmin Pārāsivi teaches his disciples development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*].”
mn152:2.8: “If that is so, Uttara, then a blind person would be one with developed *indriyas* [*bhāvitindriyo*], and a deaf person would be one with developed *indriyas* [*bhāvitindriyo*],
mn152:2.9: according to the statement of the brahmin Pārāsivi.
mn152:20.0: This is the summary of the chapters:
mn152:20.3: And the chapter on the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*]—these chapters
mn152:20.4: are found in the Upper Fifty.
mn152:20.6: Adorned with three fifties, the whole
mn152:3.1: Then the Bhagavā, knowing that Uttara, the brahmin student of Pārāsivi, sat silent, embarrassed, shoulders drooping, downcast, brooding, with nothing to say, addressed Venerable Ānanda:
mn152:3.2: “Ānanda, in one way the brahmin Pārāsivi teaches development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*], and in another way there is unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya*.”
mn152:3.3: “Now is the time, Bhagavā; now is the time, Sugata,
mn152:3.4: for the Bhagavā to teach the unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanaṁ*] in the noble *vinaya*. Having heard it from the Bhagavā, the *bhikkhus* will remember it.”
mn152:3.5: “Then listen, Ānanda, and attend closely. I will speak.”
mn152:3.6: “Yes, bhante,” Venerable Ānanda replied to the Bhagavā.
mn152:4.1: “And how, Ānanda, is there unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya*?
mn152:4.10: Just as, Ānanda, a person with good eyesight might open the eyes after closing them, or close them after opening them;
mn152:4.11: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:4.12: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding forms cognizable by the eye.
mn152:4.2: Here, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* sees a form with the eye, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:4.4: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:4.5: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:4.6: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:4.8: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:5.1: Again, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* hears a sound with the ear, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:5.10: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:5.11: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding sounds cognizable by the ear.
mn152:5.3: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:5.4: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:5.5: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:5.7: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:5.9: Just as, Ānanda, a strong person might snap the fingers with little difficulty;
mn152:6.1: Again, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* smells an odor with the nose, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:6.10: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:6.11: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding odors cognizable by the nose.
mn152:6.3: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:6.4: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:6.5: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:6.7: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:6.9: Just as, Ānanda, on a gently quivering lotus leaf, drops of water roll off and do not stay;
mn152:7.1: Again, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* tastes a flavor with the tongue, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:7.10: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:7.11: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding flavors cognizable by the tongue.
mn152:7.3: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:7.4: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:7.5: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:7.7: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:7.9: Just as, Ānanda, a strong person might gather a lump of saliva on the tip of the tongue and spit it out with little difficulty;
mn152:8.1: Again, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* touches a tangible thing with the body, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:8.10: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:8.11: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding tangible things cognizable by the body.
mn152:8.3: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:8.4: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:8.5: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:8.7: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:8.9: Just as, Ānanda, a strong person might extend a bent arm or bend an extended arm;
mn152:9.1: Again, Ānanda, when a *bhikkhu* cognizes a *dhamma* with the mind, liking arises, disliking arises, both liking and disliking arise.
mn152:9.10: Slow, Ānanda, is the dropping of the water drops, but they would quickly come to exhaustion and disappearance;
mn152:9.11: just so, Ānanda, for anyone whose arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease so swiftly, so abruptly, and so easily, and equanimity [*upekkhā*] becomes established—
mn152:9.12: this is called, Ānanda, unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya* regarding *dhammas* cognizable by the mind.
mn152:9.13: Thus, Ānanda, there is unsurpassed development of the *indriyas* [*indriyabhāvanā*] in the noble *vinaya*.
mn152:9.3: ‘This liking has arisen in me, disliking has arisen, both liking and disliking have arisen.
mn152:9.4: And that is *saṅkhata*, coarse, arisen from *paṭiccasamuppāda* [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*].
mn152:9.5: This is peaceful, this is sublime, namely—
mn152:9.7: For him, that arisen liking, arisen disliking, arisen both liking and disliking cease;
mn152:9.9: Just as, Ānanda, a strong person might drop two or three drops of water onto an iron pan heated all day.
